title: Per-slice run-ledger record — the conductor's durable state (Linear is only a human-facing index)
description: One record per slice, updated by the conductor at every transition. The ledger (campaign-scoped, e.g. orchestration/runs/<slug>/ledger.json) is the source of truth for resume: a re-run skips phase=merged and resumes the rest. /resume kills teammates, so the resume context packet is rebuilt ENTIRELY from this record — report_file, verdict_file, files_changed, and last_defects exist so the re-spawned implementer gets the prior diff + the last reviewer findings without the dead teammate.

phase.description: pending→dispatched→(changes↔dispatched)*→pass→merged (merged = into the campaign integration branch, not main). `shipped` is vestigial/unused per-slice — the only ship is the campaign-level integration→main squash. held=ESCALATE awaiting the owner; blocked=boundary breach / unrecoverable.

agent_id.description: the live impl-<slice> teammate's addressable id (for SendMessage re-entry)

worktree_path.description: the manually-created git worktree dir (the isolation flag is a no-op — see agent-teams-lifecycle-findings)

base_sha.description: recorded at dispatch; the boundary check diffs <base_sha>...HEAD

report_file.description: the implementer's last report-file path (implementer-report contract); re-read into the resume context packet

verdict_file.description: the reviewer's last verdict-file path (reviewer-report contract)

files_changed.description: files the implementer changed so far (from its report) — seeds the resume packet without re-deriving

last_defects.description: the MOST RECENT reviewer defects (CHANGES); the only place resume can read 'last findings to address' (the teammate that held them is gone). [] after a PASS verdict.

ac_evidence.description: redacted per-AC evidence the conductor used to tick Linear ACs (case_id/*_hash only)

ship_pr.description: vestigial — always null per-slice (no per-slice PR). The single campaign PR is run-ledger.campaign_pr.

updated_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp stamped by the conductor (not inside any workflow script)
